options.name: progression_balancing
options.description:
A system that can move progression earlier, to try and prevent the player from getting stuck and bored early.

A lower setting means more getting stuck. A higher setting means less getting stuck.

options.name: accessibility
options.description:
Set rules for reachability of your items/locations.

**Full:** ensure everything can be reached and acquired.

**Minimal:** ensure what is needed to reach your goal can be acquired.

options.name: logic
options.description:
Advanced logic enables some strategies that are more difficult or can risk forcing a Give Up, many of which involve Grab.
A list of these strategies can be found on the game page.

options.name: death_link
options.description: When you die, everyone who enabled death link dies. Of course, the reverse is true too.

options.name: goal
options.description:
Golden Diva: Defeat the four main passage bosses, reach the depths of the pyramid, and defeat the Golden Diva
Golden Treasure Hunt: Find the golden treasures scattered in the world, and escape through the Sound Room
Golden Diva Treasure Hunt: Find the golden treasures, and then defeat the Golden Diva
Local Golden Treasure Hunt: Find the treasures scattered in the pyramid and escape through the Sound Room
Local Golden Diva Treasure Hunt: Find the golden treasures in the pyramid and defeat the Golden Diva

options.name: golden_treasure_count
options.description: Number of treasures required to win in Golden Treasure Hunt.

options.name: difficulty
options.description:
The game's difficulty level.
Hard and S-Hard have slightly fewer locations to check since some Full Health item boxes are missing on those difficulties.

options.name: required_jewels
options.description:
Number of jewels required to fight the bosses.
The Entry Passage and Golden Pyramid always require 1 unless this option is
set to 0.

options.name: open_doors
options.description:
Start with all doors in the passages unlocked. This skips the requirement
to find Keyzer in each level, opening more locations earlier.

options.name: keyzer_shuffle
options.description:
Randomize Keyzer's location in each level.
In Open Doors, random items will be placed where Keyzer used to be.

options.name: portal
options.description:
Behavior of the portal and item collection.
Vanilla: The exit portal closes, and Wario must reopen it with the frog switch
Open: The portal stays open, allowing Wario to leave at any time.

options.name: pool_jewels
options.description:
Number of jewels in the item pool per passage for the main four.
The number of pieces will be four times this number.

options.name: golden_jewels
options.description: Number of copies of the golden pyramid jewel in the item pool.

options.name: restrict_self_locking_jewel_pieces
options.description:
Restrict extra jewel pieces from being placed on their own bosses or in the
Golden Passage in treasure hunt modes. The latter does not apply to the
golden jewel pieces.

options.name: diamond_shuffle
options.description: Shuffle the 1,000-point diamonds into the item pool.

options.name: prize_weight
options.description: How often to place prizes (full health items, diamonds) when filling vacant spots in the item pool.

options.name: junk_weight
options.description: How often to place junk items (hearts, minigame medals) when filling vacant spots in the item pool.

options.name: trap_weight
options.description: How often to place traps when filling vacant spots in the item pool.

options.name: send_locations_to_server
options.description:
When to tell the server you've found other players' items.
On Escape: Only count your locations after the game saves. If you die or give up, the items you found will be hinted.
Immediately: Count your locations as you take them from the box.
Regardless of this setting, your locations will only be sent when you escape, and sending other players items from a
level you can't clear is never in logic.

options.name: trap_behavior
options.description:
What happens if multiple traps get sent to you while you aren't playing a level.
Accumulate: Every trap you receive will be applied the next time you enter.
Apply Once: You will be forcibly transformed or hurt only once.
If you die in a level with several traps accumulated, they will be limited to one each the next time you enter.

options.name: smash_through_hard_blocks
options.description:
Break hard, teal blocks with the dash attack and super ground pound without stopping,
as in Pizza Tower and Wario Land: Shake It!
This option does not affect logic.

options.name: music_shuffle
options.description:
Music shuffle type
None: Music is not shuffled
Levels Only: Only shuffle music between the main levels
Levels And Extras: Shuffle any music that plays in levels, including the "Hurry up!" and boss themes
Full: Shuffle all music
Disabled: Disable all music

options.name: wario_voice_shuffle
options.description: Randomize the things Wario says.

options.name: local_items
options.description: Forces these items to be in their native world.

options.name: non_local_items
options.description: Forces these items to be outside their native world.

options.name: start_inventory
options.description: Start with the specified amount of these items. Example: "Bomb: 1"

options.name: start_hints
options.description: Start with these item's locations prefilled into the ``!hint`` command.

options.name: start_location_hints
options.description: Start with these locations and their item prefilled into the ``!hint`` command.

options.name: item_links
options.description: Share part of your item pool with other players.

options.name: plando_items
options.description: Generic items plando.

options.name: start_inventory_from_pool
options.description:
Start with the specified amount of these items and don't place them in the world. Example: "Bomb: 1"

The game decides what the replacement items will be.
